texto: Long ago, the gods argued over where to hide the secret to happiness, so that in finding it, the people would appreciate its importance. One god suggested a high mountain-top. Another wanted to bury it deep in some unknown land. And yet another said to hide it in the darkest waters of the ocean. Where do you suppose they hid it? They hid it in the depths of the heart. Happiness lies within one's heart. When you touch someone's heart, or your heart is touched, you feel happiness. People are made that way.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Kae Maruya que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Long ago, the gods argued over where to hide the secret to happiness, so that in finding it, the …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre felicidade, happiness, joy, com foco em reflexao-noturna e joy.
